evidence_summary: Lists database-property filling, screenshots and images, highlights, automatic page metadata mapping, and reusable forms.
Positions one-click page saving and immediate destination choice as the core job.
Lists reusable flows, property values, formatted highlights, append-to-previous capture, instant capture, and shortcuts.
Lists multi-format highlights, quick notes, collections, source context, video timestamps, PDFs, and return-to-source behavior.
Public summary praises breadth and daily convenience but reports repeated save failures, onboarding friction, Gmail failures, timestamp confusion, and shortcut conflicts.
Public summary praises speed and focus but reports offline errors, page-specific failures, missing property editing, and no remembered default destination.
Public summary praises flexible flows and daily usefulness but reports instability, login/account problems, formatting loss, and inconsistent behavior.
A recent thread reports duplicated highlights, crashes, and failed basic capture attempts; commenters also favor a simpler alternative when advanced flows are unreliable.
A recent thread reports a broken save flow after platform changes and image-selection failures, with mixed reports about whether reauthentication helps.
Documents multi-tab sidebar capture, multi-highlight collection, annotations, append-to-previous capture, property filling, and official-API access controls.
Documents that activeTab grants temporary page access only after explicit gestures such as action clicks, shortcuts, and context-menu actions.
Documents the extension Prompt API, availability checks, separate model download, supported desktop requirements, and user-activation requirement.
Documents append positioning, a 100-child limit per request, and request-limit error behavior.
Documents that page properties must conform to the parent data source schema and can be addressed by stable property IDs.
Documents cross-tab active drafts, up to 20 sources, Recent editing, durable delivery, recovery exports, and optional reviewed on-device AI.
Implements URL-deduplicated sources, cross-tab context merge, selection append, revision protection, and a 20-source cap.
Implements explicit title and to-do actions with structured output, untrusted-content framing, a 16k-character input cap, and no automatic mutation.
